content.text: What Multi-Head Attention Is
Multi-head attention
is a Transformer mechanism that runs attention
multiple times in parallel

, so the model can look at the same sequence from several different “angles” at once. Instead of using one attention calculation, it splits the work into several smaller attention heads, each with its own learned projections of Queries, Keys, and Values.[]
Why It Matters

A single attention head can miss useful relationships, because one view of the sequence may not capture everything at once. Multi-head attention fixes this by letting different heads specialize in different patterns, such as grammar, meaning, long-range dependency, or token relationships.[][]
This is one of the main reasons Transformers are so powerful in generative AI: they build
richer context representations
than a single attention pass can provide.[]
How It Works
The process is:
Start with the input embeddings.
Project them into multiple sets of Q, K, and V using different learned linear layers.
Run scaled dot-product attention independently in each head.
Concatenate all head outputs.
Apply one final linear layer to mix the information back together.[]
So each head is basically a separate attention module with its own learned perspective.[][]
Intuition
Think of multi-head attention as a
team of specialists
One head may focus on syntax.
Another may focus on meaning.
Another may track long-distance references like pronouns.
Another may notice local word patterns.[][]
After each specialist gives its opinion, the model combines them into one final representation.[]
Why Multiple Heads Help
Multiple heads help because language is not one-dimensional. The same token can participate in several relationships at once, and different heads can capture those relationships independently.[]
For example, a word can simultaneously:
refer back to a noun,
depend on nearby verbs,
and contribute to overall sentence meaning.[][]
A single head may blur these patterns together, while multiple heads can separate them more cleanly.[]
Output Shape Idea

If the model has a hidden size of 512 and uses 8 heads, each head may work on a 64-dimensional subspace, then the outputs are joined back together and transformed again. This lets the model keep the overall representation size manageable while still learning many attention patterns in parallel.[][][]
Mental Model
Imagine reading a sentence with several experts beside you:
One expert watches grammar.
One tracks names and pronouns.
One checks topic meaning.
One notices emphasis and tone.
Each expert writes a short note, and then those notes are merged into one better understanding of the sentence.[][]
Key Takeaways
Multi-head attention runs attention
in parallel across multiple heads
Each head gets its own learned Q, K, and V projections.[]
Different heads can learn different kinds of relationships in the same sequence.[][]
Head outputs are concatenated and passed through a final linear layer.[]
It gives Transformers richer representations than single-head attention.[]
This mechanism is a core reason modern generative AI models work so well.[]
